raw_response:
Tool Call: retrieve("location of spinal cord termination in adults")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

Based on the retrieved evidence, the majority of sources confirm that the adult spinal cord typically extends to the lower border of the first lumbar vertebra (L1), occasionally varying up to the upper edge of L2. This aligns with our prior conclusions and matches option B. The evidence does not suggest any substantial deviation from this normative pattern reported extensively in medical literature. Therefore, I maintain agreement with the previous conclusion.
\boxed{\text{B}}
